description: CT image-only generation without paired labels via NV-Generate-CTMR diffusion (GPU)
rules: thin wrapper: invokes upstream python -m scripts.diff_model_infer — do not reimplement diffusion sampling

MODEL_CONFIG is a diffusion_unet_inference override JSON (or 'default'); allowed keys are dim, spacing, top_region_index, bottom_region_index, random_seed, num_inference_steps, cfg_guidance_scale, output_prefix

the wrapper validates the rendered config before launching: dim xy in {256,384,512} and z in {128,256,384,512,640,768} with dim[0]==dim[1], spacing within CT ranges, x/y FOV >= 256 mm, 4-tuple region indices, and modality forced to 1
VERSION selects rflow-ct or ddpm-ct (different network/model/env configs and weights)
PREFLIGHT=1 (default) validates the config + weights/CUDA presence and previews cost without launching inference; set PREFLIGHT=0 (and CONFIRM=1 for heavy runs) to actually generate
outputs result.json plus *.nii.gz under record_store; configs are staged under the output dir so the upstream clone is never mutated
needs the CT image model weights, CUDA, and an NVIDIA GPU — there is no CPU fallback
usage: Set MODEL_CONFIG (override JSON path or 'default') and NV_GENERATE_ROOT, optionally VERSION. Default run is a config-validation + cost-preview report; pass PREFLIGHT=0 to run inference. Output: result.json with per-image HU/geometry summaries and requested-vs-actual shape/spacing checks.
limitation: Thin wrapper — do not modify code under NV_GENERATE_ROOT. Image-only synthesis produces no paired label masks (use generate for paired output). GPU-heavy and cost-gated. Output is synthetic and NOT clinically meaningful. Not for clinical deployment, interpretation, autonomous diagnosis, or regulatory submission.
